content:
- Marked the task file complete with all acceptance criteria checked and passes=true.
- Ran .ralph/task_switch.sh successfully; Ralph updated current task tracking and sent the completion email.
- Next I am reviewing the final worktree, then I will commit all changes including .ralph and push.

- Finished the task end to end and pushed commit 906f626.
- Implemented `pgtm debug verbose` with `--since`, shared deserializable debug verbose payload structs, explicit per-node debug observation states in `status -v`, richer human rendering, raw JSON payload output, and CLI-first docs rewrites.
- Required verification passed: make docs-build, make check, make test, make test-long, make lint. Post-commit docs publish hook also succeeded and pushed the mdBook sync commit to pgtuskmaster-docs.

- Read progress log and loaded .ralph/current_tasks.md plus .ralph/current_tasks_done.md.
- Active candidates currently visible are one medium-priority ctl docs task and four low-priority HA hardening tasks.
- Next I need to inspect each candidate file for blocked_by tags and any story-chain constraints before writing .ralph/current_task.txt.

- Checked all active candidate task files for blocked_by tags and priority.
- There are no active bug tasks, no blockers on the five active failing tasks, and no ultra_high story chain to preserve.
- Selected .ralph/tasks/story-ctl-operator-experience/06-task-rewrite-operator-docs-to-prefer-ctl-over-raw-curl.md because it is the highest-priority unblocked failing task (medium beats the low-priority HA hardening tasks).

- Read my previous progress log and confirmed the prior task had been completed and pushed.
- Read .ralph/current_task.txt; the active task is .ralph/tasks/story-ctl-operator-experience/06-task-rewrite-operator-docs-to-prefer-ctl-over-raw-curl.md.
- Next I am inspecting that task file to determine whether it needs initial planning, skeptical verification, or execution.

- Read the active task file for rewrite-operator-docs-to-prefer-ctl-over-raw-curl and confirmed it was still in the initial not_started state.
- Audited docs/src for operator-facing curl/jq/base-url usage and found the main rewrite targets in how-to pages, tutorial pages, and navigation/reference overview pages.
- Found the main execution trap early: the shipped Docker runtime configs only expose api.listen_addr=0.0.0.0:8080 and do not set pgtm.api_url, so host-side pgtm -c examples are not truthful until execution fixes that.
- Appended a detailed execution plan to the task file covering config-backed example truth, page-by-page doc rewrites, navigation/reference cleanup, shortening goals, and required verification.
- Ended the plan with TO BE VERIFIED and stopped without executing the task, per the workflow.
- Next step, in a fresh turn, is to perform the skeptical verification pass, alter at least one part of the plan, and replace TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE only after that review is genuinely better than the first draft.

- Read the active task file and confirmed it was in the skeptical review phase with TO BE VERIFIED.
- Re-grepped the docs and confirmed the rewrite scope is broader than the initial draft: single-node/tutorial pages, observing failover, network partition, monitor-via-metrics, add-cluster-node, TLS security, and navigation/reference overview still contain raw curl/jq or old cargo-run CLI examples.
- Verified the original config idea needed correction: the shipped docker runtime configs expose api.listen_addr=0.0.0.0:8080 and have no [pgtm].api_url, but changing those container runtime files would likely mix container-internal daemon config with host-side operator examples.
- Next I am patching the task plan to require a docs-owned example client config or tutorial-local overlay as the default execution path, plus a more explicit page inventory and grep-based verification target.

- Completed the skeptical verification pass on the operator-docs rewrite task.
- Made a substantive plan correction: execution should prefer a docs-owned operator client config or overlay with [pgtm].api_url instead of changing the shipped docker runtime configs by default.
- Tightened the execution inventory to the exact docs files still containing curl/jq/--base-url/cargo-run operator examples and replaced TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE in the task file.
- Stopping now so the next turn can execute the approved plan directly, without re-exploration.

- Read the active task file and confirmed it is in NOW EXECUTE state.
- Starting execution directly from the approved plan: rewrite the targeted docs, add a truthful operator-facing config example if needed, then run all required validation gates.
- Next I am opening the listed doc files plus runtime configuration reference and existing pgtm reference so I can patch them without another exploration pass.

- Added docs-owned operator configs under docs/examples for docker single-node and cluster node-a/node-b/node-c, each mirroring the shipped runtime config and adding pgtm.api_url for the host-mapped API port.
- Rewrote the targeted tutorials/how-to pages to use pgtm -c as the primary operator path, removed routine curl and old cargo-run pgtm usage, and tightened overview/navigation/reference framing so pgtm is clearly the operator CLI and pgtuskmaster CLI is daemon-only.
- Did the config-consistency pass too: fixed a TOML typo in the new node-c example, added a runtime-configuration note about the docs/examples pattern, and cleaned up remaining operator-facing mentions in handle-primary-failure/check-cluster-health/perform-switchover.
- Targeted grep now shows only intentional low-level reference mentions and minimal jq extraction for saved JSON sequence handling.
- Next I am running the required validation gates: make check, make test, make test-long, and make lint. After that I will update the task file, switch task, commit, and push.
